lines.en_lit: is needed for the latter clause They remove and fold them and place them under their heads.

They remove and fold them and place them under their heads. Learn from this it is permitted to derive benefit from priestly vestments. Rav Pappa said: Do not say under their heads rather, say next to their heads. Rav Mesharshiyya said: learn from here phylacteries to the side well;

So too, it is reasonable that next to their heads as, if it could enter your mind under their heads, and I would derive due to diverse kinds, as there is belt, And even if that it is permitted to derive benefit from deriving benefit from diverse kinds.

it works out well according to the one who said: The belt of the High Priest is the same as the belt of a common priest. However, according to the one who said the High Priest’s belt is not the same as the belt of a common priest, what is there to say?

And if you say diverse kinds wearing placing upon oneself is prohibited, but spreading them out is permitted, wasn’t it taught “Neither shall there come upon you But you are permitted to spread it beneath you but the Sages said: It is prohibited to do so, lest a fiber wrap upon his flesh,

And if you say placing a separation between didn’t Rabbi Shimon ben Pazi say Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi said Rabbi said in the name of the holy community in Jerusalem: Even ten mattresses one atop the other and diverse kinds underneath them it is prohibited to sleep upon them? Rather, not conclude from next to their heads? Learn from it

Rav Ashi said: Actually, under their heads. deriving benefit from diverse kinds priestly vestments, are stiff, in accordance with that Rav Huna, son of Rabbi Yehoshua, said: This stiff felt [namta], in Neresh, is permitted,

Come hear priestly vestments, it is prohibited to go out to the country, wearing them, but in the Temple it is permitted whether during the service or not during the service, due to that it is permitted to derive benefit from priestly vestments. Learn from this
not in the country? Wasn’t it taught The twenty-fifth of Tevet the day of Mount Gerizim, eulogizing not

day that the Samaritans [kutim] requested the House of our Lord from Alexander the Macedonian in order to destroy it, and he gave it to them, came and informed Shimon HaTzaddik, What did he do? He donned the priestly vestments and wrapped himself in the priestly vestments. And the nobles of the Jewish People with him, torches of fire in their hands. And all that night, these, approached from this side, and those, approached from that side, until dawn,

When dawn arrived, said to Who are these They said to him: Jews who rebelled against you. When he reached Antipatris, the sun shone and met each other. When saw Shimon HaTzaddik, he descended from his chariot and bowed before him. said to him: an important king such as you bow to this Jew? said to them: the image of this man’s face is victorious before me on my battlefields,

He said Why have you come? They said Is it possible that house in which we pray for you and for your kingdom not to be destroyed, gentiles will mislead you into destroying it, He said to them: Who are these said to him: these Samaritans who stand before you. He said to them: they are delivered into your hands

Immediately, they stabbed in their heels and hung them from their horses’ tails and continued to drag them over the thorns and thistles until they reached Mount Gerizim. When they arrived at Mount Gerizim, they plowed it over and seeded with leeks, just as they had sought to do to the House of our Lord. And they made that day a festival
If you wish, say fitting to be priestly vestments And if you wish, say “It is time to act for the Lord, they have nullified your Torah”
The synagogue attendant takes a Torah scroll Learn from here honor may be given to a student in the presence of the teacher. Abaye said: the entire is for the honor of the High Priest.
The High Priest stands By inference, he sitting. But didn’t we learn
